core_mechanism: biased competitive-aggregation (Achlioptas-type) growth of connected components on a network, where a selection rule that preferentially links elements based on current cluster sizes drives a discontinuous, first-order percolation transition — an explosive jump in the largest component's size — placing it in a distinct universality class from ordinary random aggregation
brief_reason: The abstract describes occupying bonds via a cooperative process whose choice depends on the masses of the clusters being connected, yielding a sharp first-order jump in the largest cluster's mass and a new critical universality class.
